prompt: A Meridian Content Integrity & Event Ops platform capable of ingesting third-party articles and media metadata, normalizing them into trusted internal records, and operating offline compliance, analytics, and event configuration workflows through a single-machine backend API. The service exposes Parsing APIs that accept raw HTML or plain text plus an optional source identifier and return a normalized content object with required fields for title (1–180 chars), body (min 200 chars after denoising), detected language (ISO code, required), author (optional), published_at (normalized to local server time, stored as UTC), media_source (enum), and section_tags (0–10). The parser must perform HTML denoising, character encoding correction (UTF-8 canonicalization), URL stripping, and lightweight image/video extraction by capturing embedded references and storing them as local file pointers or hashed external references without fetching remote content. Risk & Compliance APIs support creating automated and manual review decisions for text/image flags using locally defined rule packs (keyword/regex patterns, maximum ad-link density of 3 per 1,000 characters, and banned-domain lists), plus Report and Appeal operations with SLA targets (initial review within 24 business hours) and immutable moderation notes. Deduplication & ID Alignment APIs generate a global content_id and perform cross-source merging using fingerprints (normalized title + creator/author + duration when present) and fuzzy similarity thresholds (e.g., title similarity ≥ 0.92) while maintaining a mapping table from source_record_id to content_id to prevent duplicate analytics and downstream contamination. Event Lifecycle Configuration APIs manage competitions/events as versioned schedules with templates (individual/team/track), rule parameters (check-in opens 60 minutes prior, late check-in cutoff 10 minutes after start, attempt limit 3 unless overridden), advancement rules, venue/equipment bindings, publish/rollback semantics, and audit attribution for every change. Multi-role Account and Access APIs support local username + password sign-up/sign-in and password reset via security questions (no email/SMS), with roles Learner, Instructor, Reviewer, and Administrator; RBAC must enforce object-level authorization for content items, moderation cases, event definitions, and protected analytics views, with explicit deny-by-default for write operations. The backend uses Slim to structure resource-oriented APIs and Eloquent with MySQL for persistence, ensuring all processing runs fully offline in Docker on a single host. Core tables include users (username unique, password_hash with bcrypt, status enum, last_login_at), roles and permissions with group bindings, contents (content_id unique, source enums, language, published_at indexed, body_checksum, risk_state enum), content_sources (source_record_id unique per source, mapped content_id), moderation_cases (type enum, reason_code, decision, reviewer_id, appeal_state), events (event_id, version, status, effective_from), and analytics_events (actor_id, event_type enum, object_type/id, occurred_at indexed). Analytics & Governance APIs must support event ingestion (idempotency key required; duplicate keys within 24 hours rejected), funnel queries, content performance metrics (views, dwell_seconds capped at 4 hours per session), and scheduled report generation stored locally as files with retention of 90 days; sensitive fields (emails if stored, IPs) must be masked in query responses based on permission scopes. Security requirements include API rate limiting per user (default 60 requests/minute; elevated for system accounts), blacklist support (user/content/source), tamper-evident audit trails (append-only logs with daily hash chaining), and encryption at rest for secrets and security answers using AES-256 with a locally managed key. Non-functional targets: p95 API latency under 250 ms for typical reads at 200 RPS on a single machine, daily batch jobs completing within a 2-hour window, structured logs and metrics exportable to local files, and deterministic behavior without any third-party network dependencies.
